question: The concept of 'sport-specific warm-up protocols'
options: GPS, heart rate data, RPE and wellness questionnaires to optimise training
Concentrated blocks focusing on one fitness quality at a time
Combining heavy endurance and strength training may limit adaptations to both
Activate the exact neuromuscular patterns used in the subsequent activity
answer: Activate the exact neuromuscular patterns used in the subsequent activity

question: 'Foam rolling' (self-myofascial release)
options: Concentrated blocks focusing on one fitness quality at a time
GPS, heart rate data, RPE and wellness questionnaires to optimise training
Assessment of recovery status before deciding training load for that session
Applies pressure to soft tissue to reduce muscle tension and improve flexibility
answer: Applies pressure to soft tissue to reduce muscle tension and improve flexibility

question: The concept of 'periodisation models' (linear vs undulating)
options: Linear progressively increases load; undulating varies load frequently
Emphasises the lengthening phase of muscle contraction for strength and injury prevention
Assessment of recovery status before deciding training load for that session
Heavy resistance exercise with plyometric exercise for enhanced power development
answer: Linear progressively increases load; undulating varies load frequently

question: 'Tapering strategies' before competition
options: Reduce training volume while maintaining intensity to optimise performance
Emphasises the lengthening phase of muscle contraction for strength and injury prevention
Alternating heavy and light loads to stimulate neural potentiation
Applies pressure to soft tissue to reduce muscle tension and improve flexibility
answer: Reduce training volume while maintaining intensity to optimise performance

question: The concept of 'deloading weeks'
options: Planned periods of reduced training to promote recovery and prevent overtraining
Reduce training volume while maintaining intensity to optimise performance
Emphasises the lengthening phase of muscle contraction for strength and injury prevention
Assessment of recovery status before deciding training load for that session
answer: Planned periods of reduced training to promote recovery and prevent overtraining

question: 'Block periodisation' organises training into
options: Muscle contraction against immovable resistance with no change in muscle length
Concentrated blocks focusing on one fitness quality at a time
Alternating heavy and light loads to stimulate neural potentiation
Planned periods of reduced training to promote recovery and prevent overtraining
answer: Concentrated blocks focusing on one fitness quality at a time

question: The concept of 'concurrent training interference'
options: GPS, heart rate data, RPE and wellness questionnaires to optimise training
Assessment of recovery status before deciding training load for that session
Combining heavy endurance and strength training may limit adaptations to both
Activate the exact neuromuscular patterns used in the subsequent activity
answer: Combining heavy endurance and strength training may limit adaptations to both

Concentrated blocks focusing on one fitness quality at a time

question: The concept of 'contrast training'
options: Combine cutting-edge facilities, expert coaching and sports science support
Alternating heavy and light loads to stimulate neural potentiation
Heavy resistance exercise with plyometric exercise for enhanced power development
Uses cuffs to restrict venous blood return, allowing low-load training to stimulate hypertrophy
answer: Alternating heavy and light loads to stimulate neural potentiation

Heavy resistance exercise with plyometric exercise for enhanced power development
Uses cuffs to restrict venous blood return, allowing low-load training to stimulate hypertrophy
Alternating heavy and light loads to stimulate neural potentiation
